title: Do Not Stop Sea Turtle Fights

content.en: Some divers were at a beach in Xiaoliuqiu. They heard loud splashes! They saw two big things push each other. The things bit each other. They hit each other. Were monsters fighting?!
content.tw: 在小琉球的沙灘邊，幾名潛水客聽到很大的潑水聲。他們發現有兩個大東西正在互相推擠。牠們咬來咬去，攻擊對方，是怪物在打架嗎?

content.en: They were not monsters. They were sea turtles! Maybe they were fighting for space. Sea turtles like to have their own space.
content.tw: 其實牠們不是怪物，而是兩隻海龜。牠們也許在搶地盤，因為海龜喜歡有自己的空間。

content.en: The divers were worried. But they did not stop the fight. Why not? Well, in Taiwan, we cannot touch sea turtles.
content.tw: 潛水客擔心海龜受傷，並沒有出手阻止。為什麼呢？因為在台灣，人是不能碰海龜的！

content.en: Taiwan wants to keep sea turtles safe. There are not many now. Touching sea turtles can hurt them. Their skin has sticky stuff on it. When people touch this stuff, sea turtles can get sick. And they can get scared and swim into something dangerous.
content.tw: 台灣想保護為數不多的海龜。但是人類碰到海龜的皮膚，可能會導致海龜生病，或是驚嚇到牠，害牠亂了方向，撞上危險的東西。

content.en: So people who touch sea turtles may have to pay a big fine! For one turtle, the fine is three hundred thousand Taiwan dollars. For two, it is six hundred thousand Taiwan dollars! This means not touching sea turtles is very important!
content.tw: 如果有人碰了海龜，就要付高額的罰款。碰一隻海龜可能會罰到三十萬元，如果碰了兩隻，罰金還會加倍！所以，千萬不要碰海龜喔！

content.en: Sea turtles need help. This time, the divers helped by not stopping the fight!
content.tw: 海龜需要幫助。這次，潛水員們透過不阻止打鬥來提供幫助！

vocabulary.preface: 海龜非常可愛，在台灣被列為保育類動物，需要保護。

content.text: 1. swim 游泳. The sea looks perfect today! 今天的海看起來真完美! I can't wait to swim! 我等不及要游泳了! Are you sure? 你確定嗎? I heard there are turtles in this area.聽說這個區域有海龜。
2. hurt 傷害. Wait, so I can swim with turtles?! 等等，我可以跟海龜一起游!? That would be amazing! 太棒了! Don't touch them, though. You might hurt them. 可不要碰牠，可能會有傷害。
3. push 推擠. I'd never hurt a turtle! 我絕對不會傷害烏龜! I'm an animal lover! 我愛護動物! The last time I saw you, 我上次看到你， you were pushing two ants to make them fight. 你正在催兩隻螞蟻打架。
4. fight 打架. They were NOT fighting. 牠們不是在打架。They were just… talking.只是在講話。

vocabulary.postscript: 今天的單字都是動詞。swim游泳 hurt傷害 push推擠 fight打架

quiz.question: What things were fighting?
Why is it important not to touch sea turtles?
How much is the fine for touching one sea turtle?
options: c. Three hundred thousand Taiwan dollars

content.en: Today is the last day of the Lunar New Year holiday. That means it is the first day of the Taiwan Lantern Festival in Chiayi!
content.tw: 今天是農曆新年的最後一天，這也代表嘉義的台灣燈會正式開始了！

content.en: There is someone new at the festival. He is famous! He is short! He has blue eyes and brown hair! He has a big mustache under his big nose! He wears blue overalls and a red cap. The letter "M" is on his cap. Oh, and he is from a video game. Who is he?
content.tw: 今年的燈會出現了一位家喻戶曉的角色。他個子不高，有藍色眼睛、棕色頭髮，還有大鬍子！他穿著藍色吊帶褲，戴著紅色帽子，帽子上寫了字母「M」。你猜到是誰了嗎？

content.en: This famous guy is in one part of the festival. But the festival has sixteen parts! They all have amazing lanterns.
content.tw: 這個角色出現在燈會的其中一個展區。燈會一共有十六個展區，每區都有很精彩的花燈！

content.en: Some lanterns show cool stories of the Tsou people. Some show the delicious food Chiayi grows. Some show new things made by its young people.
content.tw: 有一些燈籠呈現鄒族的故事，有些介紹嘉義的在地美食，還有一些展示出年輕人的創意！

content.en: Other lanterns show how Chiayi is changing. And some show how Chiayi cares about the Earth.
content.tw: 從燈籠中，能看見嘉義的發展與轉變，一些環境保護相關的花燈也展現出嘉義人的生態關懷。

content.en: The festival has Taiwan's exciting lantern contest too!
content.tw: 燈會現場還能看到台灣花燈比賽！

content.en: You can see all this until March fifteenth. Someone wearing blue overalls and a red cap is waiting for you there! Happy Lantern Festival!
content.tw: 這些精彩內容你都能欣賞到三月十五日。穿著藍色吊帶褲、戴著紅色帽子的人正在那裡等你喔！元宵節快樂！

vocabulary.preface: 農曆春節假期之後，大家就要準備吃元宵賞花燈了。

content.text: 1. part 部分。I love Lantern Festivals! 我喜歡燈節!There's something magical about them. 它有一種魔力。Totally agree. 我完全同意。I think it's the best part of the holiday season. 我覺得它是春節最棒的部分。
2. make 製作。Last year I even saw several lanterns made by inmates.我去年還看到幾個囚犯做的花燈。Can you imagine that? 你能想像嗎?
3. change 改變。Wow, sitting in jail and making lanterns…哇，坐在監獄裡，動手做花燈...That really changed the way I think about inmates.真的讓我改變了對囚犯的想法。
4. story 故事。Yeah, it would make a great story, wouldn't it? 這會是很好的故事，不是嗎?Inmates, lanterns, light, hope…囚犯，花燈，光明，希望...

vocabulary.postscript: 你記得剛剛聽到的單字嗎?part部分 make製作 change改變 story故事

quiz.question: 1. Where is the Taiwan Lantern Festival?
2. What does the famous guy have?
3. How many parts does the festival have?

title: A Pole to Poke with Snack Sticks

content.en: The Dongnan Night Market is in Yunlin. Many food stalls there sell delicious snacks on bamboo sticks.
content.tw: 在雲林的東南夜市裡，很多攤販會賣插在竹籤上的美味小吃。

content.en: Some food stalls sell corn on sticks. Some sell meat on sticks. Others sell sweet potato balls on sticks.
content.tw: 賣烤玉米的，烤肉串的，或是地瓜球的攤販，都會用到竹籤！

content.en: When some people finish their snacks, they drop their sticks on the ground. Bamboo sticks are sharp. Other people step on them. The sticks can hurt their feet!
content.tw: 很多人吃完東西後，就把竹籤隨手丟在地上。在人來人往的夜市中，地上尖尖的竹籤可能會刺傷人！

content.en: One man saw people getting hurt. His food stall does not sell snacks on sticks. But he wanted to keep people safe. He had a smart idea. He built a tall pole with straw and wood.
content.tw: 一名攤販發現有人因為竹籤受傷，想到了一個聰明的解決辦法。他豎起一根用稻草跟木頭做的柱子！

content.en: When people finish eating, they poke their sticks into the pole. Now fewer sticks are on the ground. The night market is cleaner. Walking is safer.
content.tw: 大家吃完東西之後，可以把竹籤戳進這個柱子裡面。這樣一來，地上的竹籤減少了，夜市變得比較乾淨，走路也比較安全！

content.en: People like the pole. Many say it is simple and helpful. Some say it is fun. Others say it could be made into a straw man! The man says he will think about it.
content.tw: 大家很喜歡這個簡單、有趣的柱子，還有人提議要把柱子做成稻草人的形狀！

content.en: One man's simple idea is making the night market safer and cleaner for everyone! Now go and poke the pole!
content.tw: 一位男士的簡單想法正在讓夜市對每個人來說更安全、更乾淨！現在去戳戳那根柱子吧！

vocabulary.preface: 尖尖的竹籤可能造成傷害，用心的攤販主人想辦法改變大家的使用習慣。

content.text:
1. people 人群。
You’re late. 你遲到了。
Sorry, the night market was full of people.真抱歉，夜市擠滿了人。
I had to stand in line for everything. 每樣東西都得排隊等。

2. food 食物。
But look what I got. 不過你看我買的。
Wow, pig’s ears and chicken feet! 豬耳朵，還有雞腳!
Did you get them from that food stall I told you about?
你是在我跟你講的那一攤買的嗎?
Yep, that’s the one. 對，就是那家。

3. corn 玉米。
And I got some corn too. 我還買了幾支玉米。
Corn is the best snack! 玉米是最棒的零食!

4. sweet potato 地瓜。
I heard their sweet potatoes are good too. 聽說他們的地瓜也不錯。

vocabulary.postscript:
你喜歡逛夜市嗎?來讀單字。
people人群 food食物 corn玉米 sweet potato地瓜

quiz.question: 1. What do some people do with their sticks at the night market?
options: b. Drop them on the ground

quiz.question: 2. Why did the man make the pole?
3. What is the pole made of?

title: Man Cleans Clothes for People with No Home

content.en: Clean clothes make us feel good. They smell good. They make us look good. And they feel good on us!
content.tw: 穿著乾淨的衣服會讓人心情變好。乾淨的衣服既舒服又好看！

content.en: An American man was a police officer. He helped people. He is not a police officer now. But he wants to keep helping people. The man helps people who do not have a home. It is hard for them to wash their clothes.
content.tw: 有一名退休的美國警察喜歡幫助有需要的人，尤其是無家可歸的人，因為他們沒有辦法洗衣服！

content.en: The man has a bus. He put three washing machines and three clothes dryers in the bus. He drives to the same places every week. People know when and where to find him.
content.tw: 於是，他在一輛巴士上裝了三台洗衣機跟三台烘衣機。每個星期，他都會把公車開到固定的地點，方便大家找他洗衣服。

content.en: The man washes, dries, and folds these people's clothes for free! In about three months, the man washed more than nine hundred kilograms of clothes! And these clean clothes helped some people find a job.
content.tw: 他會免費為那些無家可歸的人洗衣服、烘乾，然後摺好！在短短的三個月內，他洗了超過九百公斤的衣服。穿上乾淨的衣服之後，有一些人甚至找到了工作！

content.en: Now the man wants another bus. He wants more washing machines and clothes dryers. He needs people to drive the bus. They will wash clothes for schoolkids who do not have a home.
content.tw: 現在，他希望有第二輛公車放更多洗衣機跟烘衣機，也想找司機來幫忙開公車。這樣一來，就能為無家可歸的學童洗衣服。

content.en: Everyone feels good wearing clean clothes. This man's idea helps many people feel good!
content.tw: 每個人穿著乾淨的衣服都會感覺很好。這名男子的想法幫助許多人感覺良好！

vocabulary.preface: 能夠穿乾淨的衣服是很大的福氣，一名好心人免費幫街友洗衣。

content.text:
1. wash 洗。
I have so many clothes to wash! 我有好多衣服要洗!
That’s exactly what a washing machine is for. 洗衣機正是做這件事用的。

2. dry 弄乾。
I know, but I still have to dry them and fold them.
我知道啊，可是我還得把它烘乾，摺起來。
Come on, let me give you a hand.來吧，我來幫你忙。

3. clean 乾淨的。
Aw, that’s so nice of you! 你真好!
I’m happy to help. 我很樂意幫忙。
Clean clothes always smell and feel good. 乾淨衣服好聞又舒服。

4. job 工作。
Come to think of it, maybe I should find a job that has something to do with
clothes. 想來我也許應該找一個 跟衣服有關的工作。

vocabulary.postscript:
還記得今天的單字嗎?
wash洗 dry弄乾 clean乾淨的 job工作

quiz.question: 1. Who does the man help?
options: a. Police officers who have a home
b. People who do not have a home
c. People who have a home

quiz.question: 2. Where did the man put the washing machines and clothes dryers?
options: b. On top of the bus

quiz.question: 3. What does the man want now?

content.en: Have you seen U.S. money? Like Taiwan's money, U.S. money is called "dollar." One U.S. dollar has 100 cents.
content.tw: 你有看過美國的錢幣嗎？美國錢叫做美元，一美元等於一百美分。

content.en: The one-cent coin is called a penny. The penny looks a lot like Taiwan's one-dollar coin. The two coins are brown. They are about the same size.
content.tw: 一美分硬幣看起來很像台灣的一元硬幣，都是咖啡色的，大小也差不多。

content.en: Some people like coins very much. They try to get new coins, old coins, or rare coins. Rare means there are almost no others. The U.S. stopped making pennies in 2025. So the last pennies are new and rare.
content.tw: 有一些人非常喜歡收集硬幣。他們會到處找新的、舊的，或是很稀有的硬幣。美國2025年停止製造一美分硬幣，所以最後出廠的硬幣不但稀有，也是全新的。

content.en: The U.S. sold the last pennies. Pennies were made for 232 years, so it sold 232 sets. One set has three pennies. Let's see… That is 696 pennies.
content.tw: 美國把最後一批一美分硬幣拿出來賣。一美分硬幣已經生產了 232 年，所以他們賣了232 套，一套有3枚硬幣，一共是 696 枚。

content.en: But one penny in each set is made of gold! That is very rare!
content.tw: 但是，每一套硬幣裡面都有一枚是用黃金做的！這些就非常稀有了。

content.en: So how much did these 696 pennies sell for? They sold for 530 million Taiwan dollars! Whoa!
content.tw: 結果這些套幣到底賣了多少錢? 總共賣了五億三千萬台幣！

content.en: Goodbye, new pennies!
content.tw: 再見了，新的一美分硬幣！

vocabulary.preface: 一分錢的美元硬幣，去年十一月停止生產，最後一批特製的套幣也賣光了。

content.text:
1. gold 黃金。
I found a gold coin in my drawer. 我在我的抽屜裡找到一枚金幣。
Real gold? 真的黃金嗎?
That’s what it says. 它上面是這麼說的。
I’m pretty sure it’s made of gold. 我確定是黃金做的。

2. size 尺寸。
It's small. 它很小。
The size of one Taiwanese dollar. 大小跟台幣一塊錢一樣。
Still, it’s gold! 但它是黃金!
Let me check my drawers too! 我也要看看我的抽屜!

3. dollar 元。
Should I sell it? 我應該賣掉嗎?
How much is it worth? 它值多少錢?
No idea. Perhaps five hundred Taiwan dollars? 不知道。也許五百塊台幣?

4. sell 賣。
A small coin that can sell for $500? 一枚小硬幣可以賣五百塊?

vocabulary.postscript: 請跟我一起讀單字。 gold黃金 size尺寸 dollar元 sell賣

quiz.question: 1. How many cents is one penny?
2. What are the last pennies?
3. How much did the last U.S. pennies sell for?

title: Man Rides Wooden Bike Down the UK

content.en: A man from Ethiopia lives in England. He was bored. He missed his family. So he tried something new. He learned how to ride a bike.
content.tw: 一名來自衣索比亞的男子現在居住在英國。他覺得很無聊，很想家，所以他學會騎腳踏車後，決定試著做一點不一樣的事情。

content.en: The man wanted to make a bike from wood. He used strong wood. He recycled a flip-flop. He made the brake with it!
content.tw: 他想用木頭做一台腳踏車！他選了很結實的木頭，還用舊拖鞋做成煞車。

content.en: A friend had an idea. The man could ride his bike from the north to the south of the UK! That is like riding down three and a half Taiwans!
content.tw: 朋友給了他建議，這名男子騎著木頭腳踏車從英國最北邊一路騎到最南邊！這段距離差不多有3.5 個台灣那麼長。

content.en: It was hard. The wind and rain were cold. Sometimes he did not have food.
content.tw: 這趟旅程十分艱難，天氣又冷又濕，有時候甚至要餓肚子。

content.en: But it was great too. Many nice new friends gave the man food and a place to stay. He stayed in homes, garages, barns, and cars! Some people sent him money for a hotel room!
content.tw: 不過，這段旅程也有很棒的地方。他在路上認識了很多新朋友，這些好心的人請他吃飯，還提供地方給他住。也有人直接給他錢，讓他住旅館！

content.en: The wooden bike looked funny. But people liked it. Sometimes it broke. People helped him fix it.
content.tw: 這台木頭腳踏車看起來奇形怪狀，但大家都很喜歡它。有時候腳踏車壞了，路人也會幫忙修理。

content.en: After a few months, the man came to the south of the UK. Friends met him with food and music. He is happy now. He has a new family of friends in the UK!
content.tw: 幾個月後，這名男子抵達了英國南部。朋友們帶著食物和音樂與他會面。他現在很快樂。他在英國有了新的朋友家人！

vocabulary.preface: 這名衣索匹亞人不但自製腳踏車，還從英國最北騎到最南邊。

content.text:
1. recycled 回收的。
Hey, have you seen that funny wooden bike outside?
你看到外面那輛怪怪的木頭腳踏車嗎?
Yes! My friend gave it to me.有啊，是我朋友送我的。
It's made from recycled wood.用回收的木頭做的。

2. brake 煞車。
Did you actually ride it? 你有真的騎它嗎?
Of course. The brake works well, so it's safe. 當然，它的煞車很管用，很安全。

3. learn 學習。
Where did your friend get the idea? 你的朋友怎麼想到這個主意?
Of making a wooden bike? 做腳踏車嗎?
He said he learned it from his family. 他說跟家人學的。

4. family 家人。
Sounds cool. 好酷喔。
A family that makes things out of wood. 會做木頭東西的家庭。

vocabulary.postscript:
你呢? 愛不愛騎腳踏車?
recycled回收的 brake煞車 learn學習 family家人

quiz.question: 1. What did the man learn?
options: a. How to ride a bike
c. How to miss his family

quiz.question: 2. Where in the UK did the man ride?
3. Who helped the man on his ride?

content.en: A Swedish musician bought a live octopus. He did not take it to his kitchen. He took it to his music studio! He put it in a big fish tank.
content.tw: 一位瑞典的音樂家買了一隻活章魚。他沒有把章魚拿去廚房，而是把牠帶到工作室，放進大水族箱裡。

content.en: The musician wanted to teach the octopus how to play music! He made a small piano. He put it in the tank. But the octopus did not play the piano.
content.tw: 音樂家想教章魚彈奏音樂。他做了一架小鋼琴，放進魚缸裡，但章魚完全不理這台鋼琴。

content.en: So the musician used bubbles. The octopus had to play a musical note. Then bubbles came out of the piano! Oooo! Soon, the octopus started to play one note. Then it played two notes.
content.tw: 音樂家想到可以用氣泡來訓練章魚。只要按下琴鍵，就會冒出氣泡。章魚覺得很有趣，於是慢慢學會按一兩個琴鍵。

content.en: But the musician wanted the octopus to play more notes. Now he uses small crabs. The octopus loves to eat them!
content.tw: 但音樂家希望章魚彈更多音符！章魚非常愛吃螃蟹，所以音樂家改用小螃蟹當做獎勵。

content.en: The octopus plays notes. It sees a crab. The crab slowly comes down a tube. Then the crab comes out!
content.tw: 只要章魚按下幾個琴鍵，就會有螃蟹從管子慢慢下來出現在牠面前，然後被章魚吃掉！

content.en: The musician taught the octopus for six months. It can play a song with him now!
content.tw: 章魚的鋼琴訓練持續了六個月。現在，章魚已經可以和音樂家合奏一首曲子了！

content.en: Does the octopus love to play the piano? Nobody knows. But it loves to eat crabs!
content.tw: 我們沒有辦法知道章魚到底喜不喜歡彈鋼琴，但可以確定的是，章魚真的很愛吃螃蟹！

content.en: Hmmm. What else can people teach an octopus?
content.tw: 嗯，人們還能教章魚什麼呢？

vocabulary.preface: 章魚據說有高智商，吸引很多人來研究。

content.text:
1. music 音樂。
An octopus knows how to play music. 有一隻章魚知道怎麼玩音樂.
Can you believe that? 你相信嗎?
No, I can't. 我不信.
It's like saying a fish can play the flute.

2. teach 教導。
But it's true! 是真的啊!
A musician taught an octopus to play the piano. 一名音樂家教會了章魚彈鋼琴.
I know octopuses are smart. 我知道章魚很聰明.

3. piano 鋼琴。
Exactly! So why not the piano? 是啊，那為什麼不能彈鋼琴?
I'll believe it when I see it. 我要看到才會相信.

4. love 喜愛。
You love playing the piano. 你喜歡彈鋼琴.
I do. 對.
And even with eight arms, it's not that easy! 就算有八隻手臂，它也不簡單!

vocabulary.postscript: 這些單字短而常用。music音樂 teach教導 piano鋼琴 love喜愛

quiz.question: 1. Where did the musician take the octopus to?
2. Which does the octopus play?
3. What does the octopus love to eat?

content.en: In a small town in the U.S., there is a restaurant near a train station. The restaurant has four tables. But it is not small. The tables are big and round.
content.tw: 美國一個小鎮的火車站旁，有一間餐廳。這間餐廳有四張圓形的大桌子。

content.en: Fifteen people can sit at each table. And, some people do not know each other! Visitors from other places sit with people from the town. Workers sit with families. Everyone meets new people.
content.tw: 每張圓桌都可以坐十五個人，來自各地的陌生人與當地人坐在一起，互相認識，也有全家人來用餐。

content.en: The waiters put food on the big Lazy Susan on each table. People spin it to get food. Some homes and restaurants in Taiwan have tables with Lazy Susans!
content.tw: 服務生把食物放在餐桌中間的轉盤上，大家可以轉動它來拿菜。台灣很多餐廳也有這種桌子，有的大家庭也使用。

content.en: The restaurant's food is hot and tasty. Many people love the fried eggplant. People eat and talk. They share stories. They laugh. They become friends!
content.tw: 餐廳的食物很好吃，炸茄子尤其受歡迎！大家邊吃飯邊聊天，談笑之間就成了朋友。

content.en: Long ago, restaurants near train stations started putting many people at one table. Lazy Susans let the restaurants feed people fast. Not many restaurants do this now.
content.tw: 從前火車站附近的餐廳經常用這種餐桌，這樣可以讓很多人更快吃飯。但是，現在這樣的餐廳現在已經不多了。

content.en: But at this restaurant, the big tables with Lazy Susans and good food keep doing one big thing. They bring people together!
content.tw: 但在這家餐廳，有轉盤的大餐桌和美味的食物持續發揮著一個重要的作用：它們讓大家聚在一起！

vocabulary.preface: 上面有轉盤的圓形大餐桌，很適合一群人共餐，但是現在不流行了。

content.text:
1. share 分享.
My mom always says, "Don't eat and talk." 我媽媽總是說，吃飯時別講話。
And you never listen. 而你從來不聽。
I can't help it. 我忍不住。
I always have stories to share! 我都有故事要分享!

2. food 食物.
Can't the stories wait until after you finish chewing?
你不能等到咀嚼完再講故事?
No! The food is hot, the story is fresh … perfect timing.
不能。菜是熱的，故事新鮮，時機正好。

3. together 一起.
So it's a combo meal? 所以是個套餐?
Exactly. Food and stories together. 正是那樣! 吃飯跟故事一起。

4. laugh 笑.
Fine. Just don't laugh when you eat and talk. 好吧，但你邊吃邊講話時可不准笑。
Please don't eat and talk at the same time.

vocabulary.postscript:
來一起讀單字。
share分享 food食物 together一起 laugh笑

quiz.question: 1. What is on the table?
2. What food do many people love at this restaurant?
3. What do people do when they are eating at this restaurant?

title: Zhongli Goat Runs Free on Streets

content.en: A goat ran this way and that way on the streets of Taoyuan!
content.tw: 有一隻山羊在桃園的路上跑來跑去！

content.en: It all began at the oldest temple in Zhongli. Renhai Temple was two hundred years old! There was lots of music! There were many dancers from Taiwan and Japan!
content.tw: 事情要從中壢最老的一間廟說起。仁海宮兩百週年那天，廟裡有音樂、也有來自台灣跟日本的舞者表演。

content.en: The temple also had a contest. It wanted to find the best goat in Zhongli!
content.tw: 廟方還辦了一場比賽，要選出中壢最好的山羊。

content.en: But that night, the winning goat ran away! It ran fast! It ran on the right side of the street! Then it ran on the left side of the street! It ran across other streets. It ran in front of cars!
content.tw: 但是當天晚上，得獎的山羊竟然逃跑了！牠一下跑到馬路右邊，一下又跑到左邊，甚至直接跑到車子前面。

content.en: The roads were dark. It was hard for drivers to see the goat! They had to turn fast! They did not want to hit it! Luckily, no one was hurt!
content.tw: 因為天色很暗，駕駛很難看清楚那隻山羊，只能緊急轉彎避開牠。不過幸好沒人受傷！

content.en: The goat's owner looked for the goat. It took forty minutes to catch it. But the goat did not want to go home. It wanted to keep running free!
content.tw: 山羊的主人花了四十分鐘才終於抓到牠。山羊好像不想回家，還想繼續在外面自由奔跑！

content.en: Renhai Temple's winning goat is home with its owner. And drivers in Taoyuan are safe from the goat... for now.
content.tw: 中壢仁海宮的得獎山羊已回到主人身邊。而桃園的駕駛們暫時不用擔心山羊會亂跑了。

vocabulary.preface: 一隻驚惶亂竄的山羊，讓主人追了好一陣子。

content.text:
1. fast 快速地.
Slow down! You are driving too fast! 慢一點，你開太快了!
Relax, I'm a great driver. 放心，我很會開車。
I just don't always know which way I'm going. 我只是不見得知道該走哪裡。

2. left 左邊的。
You should stay on the left side. 你應該待在左邊。
We are turning left in a minute. 我們馬上就要左轉了。

3. right 右邊的。
Okay, okay, I'm turning left here. 好啦，我在這裡左轉。
Wait, the hotel is on the right side! 等一下，飯店在我們右邊!
No worries, we're parking across from it! 別擔心，我們要停在它對面。

4. hit 撞上。
Whew! We're finally here! 我們終於到了!
Yeah, without hitting anyone. 對啊，沒撞到任何人。

vocabulary.postscript:
來一起讀單字。
fast快速地 left左邊的 right右邊的 hit撞上

quiz.question: 1. How old is Renhai Temple in Zhongli?
2. What kind of goat did the temple want to find?
3. When did the goat run away?

title: A Post Office Stops Delivering Letters

content.en: When was the last time you mailed a letter? Some people have never mailed a letter!
content.tw: 你上一次寄信是什麼時候呢？有一些人甚至從來沒有寄過信！

content.en: A long time ago, people mailed many letters. Post offices were busy!
content.tw: 以前大家都會寫信寄信，郵局總是很忙碌。

content.en: But now, many people send emails. Or they send texts. Not many people mail letters.
content.tw: 現在很多人改用電子郵件或傳訊息聯絡，寄信的人變得越來越少。

content.en: 25 years ago in Denmark, the post office was busy with letters. About 270 letters were mailed for each man, woman, and child! In 2024, almost 19 letters were mailed for each person.
content.tw: 二十五年前，丹麥的郵局還很忙。平均每個人一年會寄大約 270 封信。不過到了 2024 年，平均每人一年只會寄 19 封信。

content.en: But Denmark's post office is busy now too. It has more and more packages to deliver. People there love to shop online!
content.tw: 雖然信變少了，但丹麥的郵局依然很忙。這是因為當地人很喜歡用網路買東西，所以要寄送的包裹變多了！

content.en: Denmark's post office started four hundred years ago. It delivered letters and packages. But now it does not deliver letters! On December 30th, 2025, the post office delivered the last letter!
content.tw: 丹麥的郵局有四百年的歷史。本來郵局同時負責寄信和送包裹，但他們現在不再處理信件。去年 12 月 30 號郵局送出最後一封信，之後丹麥人必須靠快遞公司來送信。

content.en: Now people in Denmark cannot use their post office to mail letters. They have to use a delivery company!
content.tw: 現在丹麥的人民不能使用郵局寄信了。他們必須使用快遞公司！

vocabulary.preface: 丹麥人民要寄信，已經不能依賴郵局了。

content.text:
1. post office 郵局。
Where are you off to? 你去哪?
The post office. I need to send a package. 去郵局，我要去寄包裹。
Oh, how mysterious! 好神秘!

2. send 寄送。
I thought Valentine's Day was over. 我以為情人節已經過了。
It's not for my Valentine, okay? 不是要寄給情人好嗎?
I'm sending back a coat I bought. 我要把我買的外套寄回去。

3. never 從不。
See? That's why I never shop for clothes online.
你看，這就是為什麼我從來不上網買衣服。
You have to try things on first. 你一定得先試穿。

4. last time 上一次。
Oh, I know now. 現在我知道了。
Last time I had to send back two pairs of shoes! 上次我還得退兩雙鞋呢!

vocabulary.postscript:
你有去過郵局嗎?
post office郵局 send寄送 never從不 last time上一次

quiz.question: 1. What were Denmark's post offices busy with 25 years ago?
2. Why does this post office have more packages to deliver?
3. What do people in Denmark use to send letters now?

content.en: In the Pacific Ocean near Canada, dolphins swim. They swim in groups. They hunt for food.
content.tw: 海豚在靠近加拿大的太平洋中，成群結隊地尋找食物。

content.en: These dolphins are good at finding salmon. But they cannot catch salmon. Salmon are too big for them. The dolphins are about two and a half meters long. Salmon are about one meter long!
content.tw: 海豚很會找鮭魚，要吞下鮭魚卻有困難，因為海豚的身體沒有比鮭魚大多少。

content.en: Orcas swim nearby. They are dolphins too. But they are very big. They are seven or eight meters long! They are fast and strong.
content.tw: 虎鯨也是一種海豚，身體卻大很多，有七八公尺長，不但強壯，而且行動敏捷。

content.en: These orcas eat lots of salmon. And, they are very good at hunting for salmon. But scientists say these orcas and dolphins hunt for salmon together! The dolphins swim in front. They find a salmon. Then the orcas swim in front. They catch it! Hunting is easier together.
content.tw: 虎鯨擅長追捕鮭魚，也吃很多鮭魚。科學家注意到虎鯨跟海豚會合作，海豚游在前面，發現獵物之後虎鯨跟著捕捉。

content.en: The orcas eat first. Pieces of the salmon fall. The dolphins eat these pieces. The orcas let the dolphins stay and eat.
content.tw: 虎鯨狼吞虎嚥時，魚的碎片會落在海裡，海豚就可以享用了。

content.en: Scientists are surprised. Orcas do not share their food. But these orcas share their food with these dolphins!
content.tw: 科學家很驚訝，因為虎鯨平常不會分享食物，這些虎鯨卻願意讓海豚共餐。

content.en: Hm … Maybe these orcas can eat more salmon this way!
content.tw: 嗯...也許這些虎鯨能以這種方式吃到更多鮭魚呢！

vocabulary.preface: 科學家發現，有些虎鯨和海豚會合作獵取食物!

content.text:
1. ocean 海洋。
I dreamed of swimming with dolphins in the ocean! 我夢見跟海豚在海裡游泳!
That's a cool dream! 好酷的夢!
Isn't it wonderful? 可不是很奇妙!
I didn't want to wake up. 我都不想醒來。

2. swim 游泳。
You swam with sea turtles before. 你以前跟海龜一起游泳過。
I did. In the Galapagos. 對，在加拉巴哥群島。
They were in front of me and behind me. 牠們在我前面跟後面。

3. surprised 驚訝的。
I was very surprised to see them. 我看到牠們好驚訝!
They were not afraid of me at all! 牠們完全不怕我!

4. maybe 也許。
Maybe they wanted to be friends. 也許牠們想做朋友。
Or maybe they were trying to find out if they could eat me.
或許也想知道，能不能把我吃掉。

vocabulary.postscript:
你有沒有在海裡游泳過?
ocean海洋 swim游泳 surprised驚訝的 maybe也許

quiz.question: 1. Why can't the dolphins catch salmon?
options: b. The dolphins are too slow.
c. Orcas eat a lot of salmon.

quiz.question: 2. When hunting together, which swim in front first?
3. Why are scientists surprised?
options: b. These orcas share their food.
c. Salmon are one meter long.

title: Most People in Taiwan Need More Calcium

content.en: Do you like milk, yogurt, or cheese?
content.tw: 你喜歡牛奶、優格、或是起司嗎？

content.en: If you do, that's great! Your bones need these foods. Your teeth need them! These foods have lots of calcium.
content.tw: 這些食物裡面有滿滿的鈣質，是我們骨頭所需要的重要營養素！

content.en: Calcium is important for your body. Maybe you know it helps your bones and teeth grow. But did you know it helps your muscles move too?
content.tw: 鈣質可以讓骨頭和牙齒健康生長，也可以幫助我們的肌肉收縮。

content.en: In Taiwan, kids from one year old to three years old get all of the calcium they need. That's great!
content.tw: 在台灣，一到三歲的小朋友普遍能攝取足夠的鈣質。

content.en: But, kids from four years and up, and adults, do not get all of the calcium they need! They get half!
content.tw: 但四歲以上的小朋友跟很多大人，都有鈣質嚴重攝取不足的問題

content.en: What happens when your body needs more calcium? Maybe your bones do not grow as long or strong as they can. Maybe your teeth are not strong. Or maybe your muscles get too tight sometimes. Ouch!
content.tw: 身體缺乏鈣質會出現什麼問題呢？鈣質不足不但會影響骨頭的健康生長，讓牙齒變得脆弱，也有可能導致肌肉緊繃、痠痛！

content.en: But calcium is easy to get! Drink some milk! Eat yogurt and cheese! Black sesame and small dried fish also have lots of calcium. Many other foods do too. And sunlight helps your body take in more calcium.
content.tw: 好消息是，鈣質一點都不難取得！牛奶、優格、起司、黑芝麻、小魚乾、和很多其他的食物裡都有滿滿的鈣質。多曬太陽也可以幫助鈣質的吸收喔！

content.en: So, feed your bones, teeth, and muscles some calcium today!
content.tw: 所以，今天就為你的骨骼、牙齒和肌肉補充一些鈣質吧！

vocabulary.preface: 鈣質對身體很重要，需要多加注意。

content.text: 1. bone 骨頭。 Guess how many bones we have in our body. 猜猜看我們的身體有幾根骨頭。 Hmmm… A thousand? 一千根? Nope. 不對。 Ten thousand? 一萬根? No! We’d be too heavy!
2. muscle 肌肉。 Just tell me. 告訴我吧。 Two hundred and six. 兩百零六根。 That’s not a lot. 並不多。 Well, don't forget we also have muscles. 別忘了我們還有肌肉。
3. tooth 牙齒。 What about our teeth? 那牙齒呢? I lost one tooth yesterday! 我昨天少了一顆牙。
4. grow 生長。 That’s awful! 真糟糕! Yeah, my dentist took it out. 對啊，我的牙醫把它給拔了。 Will it grow back? 會長回來嗎? Ha ha! I wish! 哈，我也希望。

vocabulary.postscript: 來一起讀單字。 bone骨頭 muscle肌肉 tooth牙齒 grow生長

quiz.question: 1. Who gets all the calcium they need in Taiwan?
options: a. Kids from one year old to three years old
b. Kids from four years and up

quiz.question: 2. Which food has lots of calcium?
3. What helps your body take in more calcium?
